commentary:
# Cross-Exchange Liquidity Commentary — 2026-07-20

The US equity venues dominate convincingly, with Nasdaq 100 and NYSE commanding the top two positions on both price impact (0.0047% and 0.0057% per $1M traded respectively) and comparable volume ($259.6M and $163.6M). The second tier—Tokyo Stock Exchange, Euronext, and Hong Kong Stock Exchange—exhibit materially higher price impact (0.0339% to 0.0361% per $1M) but remain serviceable for institutional flow, with TSE's 3.2075% turnover ratio offsetting its modest $54.1M comparable volume. The laggards cluster sharply: Korea Exchange and London Stock Exchange suffer from both elevated ILLIQ metrics (0.1329% and 0.0770% per $1M respectively) and wider spreads, while SIX Swiss, Xetra, and LSE command only $3.2M to $8.6M in comparable volume. A notable divergence emerges at SIX Swiss, which posts the tightest AR spread at 0.5224% yet ranks sixth on ILLIQ at 0.0443% per $1M—suggesting tick-size efficiency masks underlying depth constraints. Korea Exchange presents the inverse pattern: its 1.4363% spread is substantially wider than peers, yet its 0.7532% turnover ratio hints at active domestic participation not captured by liquid-universe metrics alone.
